records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Frazzled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Frazzling (?).]

defs: To fray; to wear or pull into tatters or tag ends; to tatter; -- used literally and figuratively.
quotes: Her hair was of a reddish gray color, and its frazzled and tangled condition suggested that the woman had recently passed through a period of extreme excitement.

defs: The act or result of frazzling; the condition or quality of being frazzled; the tag end; a frayed-out end.
quotes: My fingers are all scratched to frazzles.

defs: a state of extreme exhaustion; -- often used in the phrase worn to a frazzle.
quotes: Gordon had sent word to Lee that he “had fought his corps to a frazzle.”
